name: ExtensionTestScript_Setup_Action_Assert_StopTestOnFail
title: R5: If this assert fails, will the current test execution stop? (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `TestScript.setup.action.assert.stopTestOnFail` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`TestScript.setup.action.assert.stopTestOnFail` 1..1 `boolean`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `TestScript.setup.action.assert.stopTestOnFail` 1..1 `boolean`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `TestScript.setup.action.assert.stopTestOnFail` has a context of TestScript.setup.action.assert based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `TestScript`.
Element `TestScript.setup.action.assert.stopTestOnFail` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: If this assert fails, will the current test execution stop? (new)
element.comment:
Element `TestScript.setup.action.assert.stopTestOnFail` has a context of TestScript.setup.action.assert based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `TestScript`.
Element `TestScript.setup.action.assert.stopTestOnFail` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
If this element is specified and it is true, then assertion failures should not stop the current test execution from proceeding.

element.short: If this assert fails, will the current test execution stop?
element.definition: Whether or not the current test execution will stop on failure for this assert.
element.comment: If this element is specified and it is true, then assertion failures should not stop the current test execution from proceeding.
